texts.value: Involved in the post-translational conjugation of arginine to the N-terminal aspartate or glutamate of a protein (PubMed:34893540). This arginylation is required for degradation of the protein via the ubiquitin pathway (PubMed:34893540). Does not arginylate cysteine residues (By similarity)

reaction.name: an N-terminal L-alpha-aminoacyl-[protein] + L-arginyl-tRNA(Arg) = an N-terminal L-arginyl-L-aminoacyl-[protein] + tRNA(Arg) + H(+)

texts.value: Monomer. Interacts with LIAT1; LIAT1 is not a substrate of ATE1, the interaction takes place in the cytoplasm and seems to increase ATE1 arginyltransferase activity
It is uncertain whether Met-1 or Met-37 is the initiator
